description: We support the Prime Minister and ensure the effective running of government. We are also the corporate headquarters for government, in partnership with HM Treasury, and we take the lead in certain critical policy areas. The Cabinet Office is a ministerial department, supported by 29 agencies and public bodies .

details.body:
We support the Prime Minister and ensure the effective running of government. We are also the corporate headquarters for government, in partnership with HM Treasury, and we take the lead in certain critical policy areas.

The Cabinet Office is a ministerial department, supported by 29 agencies and public bodies.

ordered_featured_documents.summary: The Government has launched a consultation asking the public to share their views on how digital ID is used to make public services quicker, easier and more secure to access.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Government launches consultation on making public services quicker, easier and more secure to access with digital ID

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Chief Secretary to the Prime Minister Darren Jones’ speech on rewiring Whitehall and building the new digital state.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Move fast. Fix things.

ordered_featured_documents.summary: On Tuesday 20 January, Chief Secretary to the Prime Minister Darren Jones gave a keynote speech to set out his plan to rewire Whitehall and modernise public services, to move fast and fix things after years of low public sector productivity.
ordered_featured_documents.title: “Move fast, fix things” – Darren Jones sets out plan to rewire Whitehall and incentivise innovation in the civil service

ordered_featured_documents.summary: The appointment marks the first time a woman has held the role in its over 100 year history.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Dame Antonia Romeo appointed as first female Cabinet Secretary and Head of the Civil Service to drive change and implement the government’s agenda

Our Personal information charter explains how we treat your personal information. Read our policy on Social media use.

details.body:
The role of Permanent Secretary carries with it the responsibility of Principal Accounting Officer, ensuring that the resources authorised by Parliament are used for the purposes authorised by Parliament.

Other responsibilities include:

  • agreeing with the Home Secretary the overarching strategy for delivering ministerial objectives
  • leading, and managing the department to ensure that it operates as effectively and efficiently as possible
  • reporting to the Home Secretary and to Parliament on overall performance

role_appointments.title: Dame Antonia Romeo DCB - Permanent Secretary

details.body: The Cabinet Secretary is the Prime Minister’s most senior policy adviser and acts as Secretary to the Cabinet, responsible to all ministers for the running of Cabinet Government.

role_appointments.title: Dame Antonia Romeo DCB - Cabinet Secretary

details.body:
The Head of the Civil Service leads nearly half a million public servants who work in public institutions, administer tax, benefits and pensions systems and put government policy into practice. The civil service is a permanent, politically impartial workforce that serves the government of the day, while retaining the flexibility to serve future governments.

Currently civil servants are supporting the government’s economic and public service reform. The scale of the challenges and persistent weaknesses require a reform plan that applies right across the civil service. The Head of the Civil Service is one of several senior civil servants accountable for the reform of the civil service through the Civil Service Board.

role_appointments.title: Dame Antonia Romeo DCB - Head of the Civil Service

details.body:
The role of Permanent Secretary carries with it the responsibility of Principal Accounting Officer, ensuring that the resources authorised by Parliament are used for the purposes authorised by Parliament.

Other responsibilities include:

  • agreeing with the Secretary of State the overarching strategy for delivering Ministerial objectives
  • leading, and managing the Ministry to ensure that it operates as effectively and efficiently as possible
  • reporting to the Justice Secretary and to Parliament on overall performance

role_appointments.title: Dame Antonia Romeo DCB - Permanent Secretary

details.body: The Permanent Secretary is responsible for leading the Department for International Trade, and is the Secretary of State’s principal policy adviser.

role_appointments.title: Antonia Romeo - Permanent Secretary

details.body: The Director-General Economic and Commercial Affairs USA sits on the Board of Department for International Trade, the foreign commercial arm of the UK government, and leads its operations across the USA.

role.title: Director-General Economic and Commercial Affairs USA

role_appointments.title: Antonia Romeo - Director-General Economic and Commercial Affairs USA

details.body:
The Consul General is the senior UK official in a Consulate General, which is a subordinate office to the Embassy or High Commission, usually located in another major city. The Consul General represents the UK government and is typically responsible for consular, visa and trade activities in their city or region.

The Consul General leads the government’s work promoting UK economic, commercial, political and consular interests in New York, Pennsylvania, New Jersey and Fairfield County, Connecticut.

British Consulate General New York

role.title: British Consul General to New York

role_appointments.title: Antonia Romeo - British Consul General in New York

role.title: Government's Special Envoy to the U.S. technology companies

role_appointments.title: Antonia Romeo - Government's Special Envoy to the U.S. technology companies

details.body:
The Director General heads the Economic and Domestic Secretariat in the Cabinet Office, which supports the Prime Minister and the Cabinet in taking forward the government’s economic and domestic priorities.

The Director General is also responsible for ensuring that Government decisions are based on the best possible data and strategic analysis, working with colleagues across the Cabinet Secretariat, No.10, all departments, and external experts, and by facilitating collective decision-making through Cabinet and its committees.

role.title: Director General, Economic and Domestic Secretariat

role_appointments.title: Antonia Romeo - Director General, Economic and Domestic Secretariat

details.body:
The group led by the Director General, Criminal Justice has responsibility for:

  • CJS policy and strategy
  • Victim and criminal proceedings policy
  • CJS services (including for victims)
  • CJS Reform projects
  • Sentencing and penalties
  • Rehabilitation
  • Offender policy
  • Women offenders
  • Youth justice
  • MoJ strategy
  • Civil Service Reform, including open/agile policy making
  • Structures and Services Review (SSR)

role_appointments.title: Antonia Romeo - Director General, Criminal Justice

details.body:
The role of the Second Permanent Secretary is to support the Permanent Secretary in the running and oversight of the department.

Responsibilities include:

  • oversight of policies to create sustainable growth across the UK

role_appointments.title: Cat Little - Second Permanent Secretary

details.body: The Chief Operating Officer for the Civil Service is responsible for ensuring the high performance and efficiency of Government, and oversees the cross-cutting Government functions to build greater professionalism and expertise across the Civil Service. The Cabinet Office Permanent Secretary is the principal accounting officer and Civil Service head of the Cabinet Office, overseeing the department and ensuring it delivers for the Prime Minister, Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster, and wider Cabinet.

role.title: Chief Operating Officer for the Civil Service and Permanent Secretary to the Cabinet Office

role_appointments.title: Cat Little - Permanent Secretary (Cabinet Office)

role.title: Head of the Government Finance Function

role_appointments.title: Cat Little - Head of the Government Finance Function
Cat Little - Acting Permanent Secretary

details.body:
Responsibilities include:

  • ensuring the delivery of HM Treasury’s work programme, in particular supporting the government’s objectives to ensure effective control of and value for money in public spending and promote sustainable economic growth
  • leading the Treasury’s spending relationship with No 10 and the Cabinet Office
  • supporting spending directors and teams in their relationships with integral Whitehall departments and with the think tank and finance communities

role_appointments.title: Cat Little - Director General, Public Spending

role.title: Interim Head of the Government Finance Function

role_appointments.title: Cat Little - Interim Head of the Government Finance Function

details.body: This role has been created by changes under the Defence Reform programme. Responsibilities will be updated in due course.

role_appointments.title: Cat Little - Director General Finance

details.body: The director is responsible for overseeing finance, IT, business change, performance, business planning and assurance.

role_appointments.title: Cat Little - Director, Finance and Performance

details.body:
The director is responsible for HR in the Cabinet Office and oversees:

  • Delivery of HR services (including a shared service model for employee and line manager transactions and casework)
  • Diversity and inclusion
  • Workforce planning
  • Resourcing
  • Employee relations and HR policy
  • Reward
  • Developing capability and talent

role_appointments.title: Christina Duncan - Chief People Officer
Ross Maude - Chief Digital and Information Officer
Ross Maude - Chief Digital and Information Officer

details.body:
The Director of Digital and Technology leads the Digital and Technology directorate. 

The role includes: 

  • developing and carrying out the digital and technology strategy
  • collaborating across the organisation to create and deliver digital services
  • identifying commercial opportunities for the directorate that deliver the most value for taxpayer money
  • managing strategic commercial relationships and delivery arrangements

role_appointments.title: Ross Maude - Director of Digital, Data and Technology

details.body: Director General for the Economic and Domestic Secretariat (EDS) at Cabinet Office

role_appointments.title: Juliet Chua CB - Director General (EDS), Cabinet Office

details.body:
The director general is a member of the Department for Education’s leadership team.

The role is responsible for:

  • the school system
  • the teaching and school support workforce
  • the curriculum
  • SEND and alternative provision
  • exams, national assessment and general qualifications
  • school accountability
  • pupil wellbeing and enrichment
  • attendance, behaviour and exclusion
  • school food
  • wraparound childcare, the Holiday Activities and Food (HAF) programme, and breakfast clubs

The director general works with the Teaching Regulation Agency.

The Chief Executive of the Standards and Testing Agency reports to the Director General, Schools.

role_appointments.title: Juliet Chua CB - Director General, Schools

details.body:
The Director General, Finance and Corporate, is responsible for:

  • people
  • finance
  • UK overseas network
  • estates and security
  • information and digital
  • commercial
  • audit and investigation
  • Protocol
  • FCDO Services

As a board member, the Director General also gives corporate leadership to the FCDO by ensuring that the department meets the international policy priorities, Public Service Agreements targets and service delivery targets set by ministers.

role_appointments.title: Juliet Chua CB - Director General, Finance and Corporate

details.body: The Director General for Finance and Corporate Performance is responsible for the following areas: finance, control and assurance; strategy; procurement and commercial activity; programme management oversight; human resources; business solutions (IT); security and facilities.

role.title: Director General, Finance and Corporate Performance

role_appointments.title: Juliet Chua - Director General, Finance and Corporate Performance

details.body:
The director general is a member of the Department for Education Board and attends both the Performance and Management Committees. They are the principal adviser on issues relating to:

  • teaching and the teaching workforce
  • the curriculum, assessment and qualifications
  • school accountability
  • early years
  • disadvantage, special education needs (SEN) and character

The Chief Executive of the Standards and Testing Agency and Director of the National College for Teaching and Leadership both report to the Director General, Education Standards.

role_appointments.title: Juliet Chua - Director General, Education Standards

details.body:
The Government Chief Security Officer (GCSO) leads the Government Security Function, which seeks to build the capacity and capabilities of security professionals across UK government departments, covering physical, human and information security.

The GCSO is also Director General of the Government Security Group within the Cabinet Office, which is responsible for the oversight, coordination and delivery of protective security within all central government departments, their agencies and arms-length bodies.

The GCSO is also responsible for, and heads, the Government Security Profession, bringing together security professionals from across government in supporting them gain skills and knowledge to fulfil their roles.

role_appointments.title: Vincent Devine - Government Chief Security Officer

details.body:
The Permanent Secretary for Communications is responsible for transforming how the UK Government communicates and builds trust with the public. It is also the head of the Government Communications profession.

Responsibilities include:

  1. Delivering a government communication strategy that reflects the evolving media landscape and technological changes.
  2. Leading major cross-government communications to support the Prime Minister’s Plan for Change, ensuring the public gets clear, timely and engaging information.
  3. Modernising how the government communicates by building capability, driving innovation and ensuring that Government Communications operates effectively and efficiently.

role_appointments.title: David Dinsmore - Permanent Secretary Director of Communications

details.body: The Chair is responsible for setting the strategic direction and aims of GCA, and supporting the CEO in making decisions that enable the organisation to achieve those aims.

role_appointments.title: Andrew Forzani - Non-Executive Chair, Government Commercial Agency

details.body:
Reporting to the Chief Executive of the Civil Service, the Government Chief Commercial Officer shapes and leads the programme of commercial reform across government.

The Government Chief Commercial Officer’s priorities include:

  • Leading the Government Commercial Function and Governments Grants Management Functions (GCF and GGMF)
  • To steer the cross-government commercial and grants communities in delivering the Plan for Change
  • Delivering efficiencies and savings through the use of digital, data and AI
  • Lead the Functional Strategy
  • Leads the relationship with HMG Strategic Suppliers
  • Improving contract delivery and contract management
  • Advancing the capability of the GCF and GGMF
  • Head of Profession, building a commercial and grants community across the UK

The Government Chief Commercial Officer works closely with departmental ministers, the Cabinet Secretary, departmental permanent secretaries and other senior officials, and senior leaders of government suppliers. The Government Chief Commercial Officer gives evidence to Parliamentary Select Committees, including the Public Accounts Committee, on commercial matters.

role_appointments.title: Andrew Forzani - Government Chief Commercial Officer

details.body: The Director General Commercial role encompasses the whole of Commercial across Defence and DE&S, and has accountability for the largest commercial workforce in the Government Commercial Function.

role_appointments.title: Andrew Forzani - Director General Commercial

details.body:
The National Security Adviser’s responsibilities include:

  • Providing advice to the Prime Minister and the Cabinet on national security matters. 
  • Attending the National Security Council.
  • Overseeing cross-government delivery of the National Security Strategy 2025.

role_appointments.title: Jonathan Powell - National Security Adviser

details.body:
The Joint Intelligence Committee (JIC) is a cross-government committee, based at the Cabinet Office, providing ministers and senior officials with co-ordinated inter-departmental intelligence assessments, primarily about security, defence and foreign affairs. It supports the National Security Council.

The Chair of the Joint Intelligence Committee is responsible to the Prime Minister for supervising the work of the JIC. The Chair is the head of the Joint Intelligence Organisation, which includes the Assessments Staff, a team of analysts responsible for drafting all-source intelligence assessments, and Professional Head of Intelligence Assessment.

role.title: Chair of the Joint Intelligence Committee

role_appointments.title: Madeleine Alessandri CMG - Chair of the Joint Intelligence Committee

details.body:
The Permanent Secretary leads the day-to-day running of the department and heads the senior leadership team. She is responsible and accountable to Parliament for the stewardship of the department’s budget and ensuring value for money as the Principal Accounting Officer.

The Permanent Secretary is the Chief Policy Adviser to the Secretary of State for Northern Ireland and the ministerial team.

Along with the departmental leadership team, the Permanent Secretary is responsible for:

  • Setting standards within the department
  • Ensuring good governance, assurance, and performance and risk management
  • Overseeing the delivery of the Department’s Outcomes Delivery Plan
  • Assuring the performance of sponsored organisations that are directly funded by the NIO
  • Approving the department’s resource accounts and departmental annual report.

role.title: Permanent Secretary for the Northern Ireland Office

role_appointments.title: Madeleine Alessandri CMG - Permanent Secretary for the Northern Ireland Office

details.body:
The Deputy National Security Advisers supports and advises the Prime Minister and National Security Adviser on:

  • countering hybrid threats
  • cyber security and the delivery of the National Cyber Security Programme
  • counter-terrorism
  • serious organised crime
  • crisis management
  • civil contingencies
  • electoral security
  • intelligence policy
  • funding of the Security and Intelligence Agencies
  • science and technology
  • foreign direct investment
  • diversity and Inclusion in the national security community

role_appointments.title: Madeleine Alessandri - Deputy National Security Adviser

details.body:
The role of the Cabinet Office Lead NEBM is to provide strategic oversight and leadership for the department’s team of Non-Executives. The Lead NEBM supports ministers and officials by providing expert advice and challenge on delivery and performance. As well as their formal role on the Board and its sub-committees, the Lead NEBM also maintains close working relationships with the Permanent Secretary and the Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster to support the delivery of their priorities. 

The Lead NEBM works with the department to ensure the NEBMs are assigned to work on issues where they will have the most impact and can best support the delivery of the department’s strategic priorities.

role_appointments.title: John Fallon - Lead Non-Executive Board Member

details.body: A non-executive director brings an external perspective and provides advice, challenge and support to the board.

role_appointments.title: Maura Sullivan - Non-Executive Director, Government Commercial Agency
Maura Sullivan - Non-Executive Director

details.body: The Cabinet Office Audit and Risk Committee offers an independent perspective on the department’s financial, risk, and control arrangements. It also reviews and recommends the approval of accounts for the Cabinet Office, Office of the Registrar of Consultant Lobbyists, Civil Superannuation, and the Royal Mail Statutory Pension Scheme.

role.title: Audit and Risk Committee Chair and Non-Executive Board Member

role_appointments.title: Maura Sullivan - Audit and Risk Committee Chair and Non-Executive Board Member

details.body:
CustomerFirst is a unit within Department for Science, Innovation and Technology (DSIT) to shape how government services are designed, delivered and run.

The Co-Chair is an unpaid advisor who shapes strategy and shares expertise, provides constructive challenge, supports on recruitment of top-tier talent, and champions the mission.

role_appointments.title: Greg Jackson CBE - Co-Chair of CustomerFirst

details.body:
The Cabinet Office Board provides strategic leadership for the department, comprising Cabinet Office ministers, senior executives, and non-executives from outside government. Its purpose is to advise on strategy, monitor performance, and assess significant risks.

The Non-Executive Board Members are responsible for providing support and challenge to the department’s ministers and senior officials on the delivery of key policies and programmes.

role_appointments.title: Greg Jackson CBE - Non-Executive Board Member

details.body: Members of the Industrial Strategy Advisory Council (ISAC) contribute to the development and long-term delivery of HM Government’s industrial strategy. They work with the ISAC chair and other ISAC members to produce independent, evidence-based recommendations that directly inform government policy.

role_appointments.title: Greg Jackson CBE - Industrial Strategy Advisory Council member

details.body:
The CFO is responsible for financial management assurance across the department, striving to ensure that the Cabinet Office gets the best return on its resources.

The CFO leads the Commercial and Portfolio function in the Cabinet Office. The CFO also manages the Security teams within the Cabinet Office, the Cabinet Office controls team as well the departmental enterprise platform.

The CFO’s policy remit is to convene the work on functional reform and impact and professional standards.

role_appointments.title: Caroline Patterson - Chief Financial Officer and Director of Assurance, Finance and Controls, Cabinet Office

details.description:
You can make a subject access request for yourself, or on behalf of someone else, online. To do this you will need a letter of consent from the individual the request relates to or confirmation of power of attorney.

Read about your personal data rights as a data subject and what can be requested: 

https://www.gov.uk/government/organisations/cabinet-office/about/personal-information-charter  
Also please find the information in regards to the  purposes for processing your personal data: https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/freedom-of-information-request-and-subject-access-request-privacy-notice/freedom-of-information-request-and-subject-access-request-privacy-notice

image.alt_text: The Rt Hon Sir Keir Starmer KCB KC MP

details.body: As Minister for the Union, the Prime Minister works to ensure that all of government is acting on behalf of the entire United Kingdom: England, Northern Ireland, Scotland, and Wales.

role_appointments.title: The Rt Hon Sir Keir Starmer KCB KC MP - Minister for the Union

details.body:
The Minister for the Civil Service is responsible for regulating the Civil Service.

The Civil Service (Management Functions) Act of 1992, allows the Minister for the Civil Service to delegate power to other ministers and devolved administrations.

This role was created in 1968 and is always held by the Prime Minister.

role_appointments.title: The Rt Hon Sir Keir Starmer KCB KC MP - Minister for the Civil Service

details.body:
The First Lord of the Treasury is one of the Lords Commissioners of the Treasury. This role is usually held by the Prime Minister.

Since the 17th century, the Lords Commissioners of the Treasury have collectively carried out duties that were previously held by the Lord High Treasurer (head of His Majesty’s Treasury).

The Lords Commissioners of the Treasury also include:

  • the Second Lord of the Treasury - the Chancellor of the Exchequer, who has most of the functional financial responsibilities
  • Junior Lords Commissioners of the Treasury - other members of the government, usually government whips in the House of Commons

10 Downing Street is the official residence of the First Lord of the Treasury, and not of the Prime Minister.

role_appointments.title: The Rt Hon Keir Starmer - First Lord of the Treasury

details.body:
The Prime Minister is the leader of His Majesty’s Government and is ultimately responsible for the policy and decisions of the government. 

As leader of the UK government the Prime Minister also: 

  • oversees the operation of the Civil Service and government agencies 
  • chooses members of the government 
  • is the principal government figure in the House of Commons

role_appointments.title: The Rt Hon Keir Starmer - Prime Minister

ordered_ministers.title: The Rt Hon Sir Keir Starmer KCB KC MP

image.alt_text: The Rt Hon Darren Jones MP

details.body: The Chief Secretary to the Prime Minister oversees work across Government to support the delivery of the Prime Minister’s priorities and the Government’s Plan for Change.

role.title: Chief Secretary to the Prime Minister

role_appointments.title: The Rt Hon Darren Jones MP - Chief Secretary to the Prime Minister

details.body: As Minister for Intergovernmental Relations, the role holder leads coordination with the devolved administrations on the Prime Minister’s behalf, working closely with the Territorial Offices, to make sure that across government work is being done on behalf of the entire United Kingdom: England, Northern Ireland, Scotland, and Wales.

role_appointments.title: The Rt Hon Darren Jones MP - Minister for Intergovernmental Relations

details.body:
The Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster administers the estates and rents of the Duchy of Lancaster, and is a member of the Cabinet.

He is responsible for:

  • Oversight of all Cabinet Office policy
  • Coordination and delivery of cross-government priorities
  • Oversight of national security policy coordination
  • Oversight of state reform and Civil Service reform
  • Communications policy for Government 
  • Propriety and ethics 
  • Public appointments

role.title: Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster

role_appointments.title: The Rt Hon Darren Jones MP - Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster

details.body:
The Chief Secretary to the Treasury (CST) is responsible for public expenditure including spending reviews and strategic planning; in-year spending controls; and driving efficiency and value for money.

Specific areas of focus in relation to public expenditure includes:

  • Treasury’s interest in mission delivery and public service reform (noting that responsibility for the Growth Mission sits with the Chancellor of the Exchequer)

  • Public sector pay

  • Annual Managed Expenditure and welfare spending

  • Tax credits policy

  • Childcare, including tax free childcare

  • Infrastructure spending

  • Digital and technology spending

  • Treasury interest in devolution to Scotland, Wales and Northern Ireland

  • Public Works Loan Board

The CST also has responsibility for capital investment, driving private sector investment in infrastructure and value for money on large national infrastructure projects. This includes being the Treasury lead on housing, planning and infrastructure reform. The CST is the responsible minister for the National Infrastructure and Service Transformation Authority.

role_appointments.title: The Rt Hon Darren Jones MP - Chief Secretary to the Treasury

ordered_ministers.title: The Rt Hon Darren Jones MP

image.alt_text: The Rt Hon Nick Thomas–Symonds MP

details.body:
The Minister for the Cabinet Office is the deputy to the Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster in all departmental business, including on inter-governmental relations and public appointments.

He is responsible for:

  • EU Policy and relations including the Windsor Framework
  • Oversight of functions and services
  • Civil Service reform
  • Inquiries policy 
  • Infected blood inquiry 
  • Duty of candour
  • Constitution and House of Lords reform 
  • Cabinet Office business planning and performance 
  • Communications function & reform
  • Public sector workforce

role.title: Minister for the Cabinet Office (Minister for the Constitution and European Union Relations)

role_appointments.title: The Rt Hon Nick Thomas–Symonds MP - Minister for the Constitution and European Relations
The Rt Hon Nick Thomas–Symonds MP - Paymaster General

ordered_ministers.title: The Rt Hon Nick Thomas–Symonds MP

details.body:
The Minister’s responsibilities are:

  • Supporting CDL with coordination of national security policy
  • Resilience 
  • Civil contingencies
  • Integrated security fund
  • Investment security
  • Cyber security
  • Government security group, including UK security vetting

role_appointments.title: Dan Jarvis MBE MP - Minister of State

details.body:
The Minister’s responsibilities are:

  • counter terrorism and extremism  
  • state threats 
  • cyber security and crime 
  • serious and organised crime 
  • oversight of the National Crime Agency 
  • international criminality 
  • aviation and maritime security 
  • economic crime (excluding fraud) 
  • extradition 
  • exclusions and deprivations 
  • MP security and VIP protection 
  • online safety 
  • anti-corruption

role_appointments.title: Dan Jarvis MP - Minister of State

image.alt_text: The Rt Hon Anna Turley MP

role.title: Junior Lord of the Treasury (Government Whip)

role_appointments.title: Anna Turley MP - Junior Lord of the Treasury (Government Whip)

details.body:
She is responsible for supporting the Chief Secretary to the Prime Minister and the Minister for the Cabinet Office on

  • Public bodies
  • Government property function and the Places for Growth programme
  • Government people group

role_appointments.title: Anna Turley MP - Minister of State (Minister without Portfolio)

ordered_ministers.title: The Rt Hon Anna Turley MP

details.body:
The Parliamentary Secretary is responsible for supporting the Chief Secretary to the Prime Minister and the Minister for the Cabinet Office on coordination and delivery of cross-government priorities.

In particular, the Minister’s responsibilities are to support the Chief Secretary to the Prime Minister and the Minister for the Cabinet Office on:

  • EU Reset and the Windsor Framework
  • Government commercial function
  • Infrastructure delivery
  • Union and constitutional matters
  • Fiscal events and departmental business planning
  • Transparency and freedom of information policy

role_appointments.title: Chris Ward MP - Parliamentary Secretary
Satvir Kaur MP - Parliamentary Secretary
James Frith MP - Parliamentary Under-Secretary of State
James Frith MP - Parliamentary Secretary
Baroness Anderson of Stoke-on-Trent - Parliamentary Secretary
Baroness Anderson of Stoke-on-Trent - Baroness in Waiting (Government Whip)

details.body:
The Prime Minister is the leader of His Majesty’s Government and is ultimately responsible for the policy and decisions of the government. 

As leader of the UK government the Prime Minister also: 

  • oversees the operation of the Civil Service and government agencies 
  • chooses members of the government 
  • is the principal government figure in the House of Commons
Responsibilities of the Minister without Portfolio include contributing to the government’s policy and decision making processes.

• Delivery of the government’s efficiency programme
  • Civil Service Modernisation and Reform
  • Places for growth programme
  • Cabinet Office business planning and performance
  • Infected Blood Inquiry 
  • Public bodies reform programme
  • Spend controls reform
  • Oversight of the cross-cutting functions and government functional strategy

Supporting the Deputy Prime Minister on:

  • Driving delivery of the government’s priorities 
  • Civil contingencies and resilience

The minister is responsible for:

  • developing government policies with the Cabinet Office, as set out in the programme for government
  • implementing departmental business plans

The Minister has responsibility for political and constitutional reform in support of the Deputy Prime Minister.

Other responsibilities include:

  • cyber security
  • civil contingencies
  • working with the Minister for the Cabinet Office on public sector efficiency and reform

ordered_roles.title: Minister for Political and Constitutional Reform

details.body:
The Minister for Civil Society leads on the civil society agenda. The minister is responsible for:

  • the Big Society agenda
  • National Citizen Service and youth policy
  • social action
  • civil society sector support
  • social enterprise and social investment

The minister is also responsible for UK statistics, and supports the Minister for the Cabinet Office on public sector efficiency and reform.

The Permanent Secretary is the head of the department, responsible for the leadership and governance of the Cabinet Office. The Permanent Secretary is also the Cabinet Office’s Accounting Officer, responsible for the financial propriety of the department.

First Parliamentary Counsel oversees the Government in Parliament Group, which includes Parliamentary Counsel and the Business Managers in each House of Parliament. The Group works with teams across government to prepare and deliver the government’s legislative programme.

ordered_roles.title: Permanent Secretary and First Parliamentary Counsel

details.body: The Cabinet Secretary is the Prime Minister’s most senior policy adviser and acts as Secretary to the Cabinet, responsible to all ministers for the running of Cabinet Government.

The Head of the Civil Service leads nearly half a million public servants who work in public institutions, administer tax, benefits and pensions systems and put government policy into practice. The civil service is a permanent, politically impartial workforce that serves the government of the day, while retaining the flexibility to serve future governments.

Currently civil servants are supporting the government’s economic and public service reform. The scale of the challenges and persistent weaknesses require a reform plan that applies right across the civil service. The Head of the Civil Service is one of several senior civil servants accountable for the reform of the civil service through the Civil Service Board.

The Director General, Propriety and Constitution Group is responsible for:

  • ensuring the highest standards of propriety and ethics across all government departments
  • overseeing constitutional policy and providing advice to Ministers on parliamentary, crown and other constitutional issues
  • overseeing the Honours Secretariat
  • overseeing the Privy Council Office
  • advising on the policies and codes governing ministers and special advisers
  • managing government’s relationship with the ‘independent offices’ (e.g. the Civil Service Commission)
  • advising on public appointments (i.e. appointment to posts of public administration)

ordered_roles.title: Director General, Propriety and Constitution Group (PCG)

details.body: The finance director provides professional financial management for the department. The director also oversees all IT, buildings and facilities management for the Cabinet Office.

The Director General heads the Economic and Domestic Secretariat in the Cabinet Office, which supports the Prime Minister and the Cabinet in taking forward the government’s economic and domestic priorities.

The Director General is also responsible for ensuring that Government decisions are based on the best possible data and strategic analysis, working with colleagues across the Cabinet Secretariat, No.10, all departments, and external experts, and by facilitating collective decision-making through Cabinet and its committees.

ordered_roles.title: Director General, Economic and Domestic Secretariat

details.body: Responsibilities include advising the Prime Minister on issues including economic strategy.

The minister supports effective coalition government working and policy development, with a particular focus on domestic policy.

He leads on carrying out the programme for government and departmental business plans.

ordered_roles.title: Minister of State for Cabinet Office

details.body:
The director is responsible for HR in the Cabinet Office and oversees:

  • Delivery of HR services (including a shared service model for employee and line manager transactions and casework)
  • Diversity and inclusion
  • Workforce planning
  • Resourcing
  • Employee relations and HR policy
  • Reward
  • Developing capability and talent

The Principal Private Secretary and Director General is responsible for ensuring the Prime Minister’s Office provides the Prime Minister with first class support, enabling the PM to establish and deliver the government’s overall strategy and policy priorities.

The Civil Service Reform Plan sets out a series of specific and practical actions to equip a much smaller civil service to meet current and future challenges.

The Director General is responsible for the implementation of the plan, working across government to ensure real and sustained change.

The Chief Operating Officer leads the efficiency and reform agenda, improving the way government operates, making efficiency savings and supporting UK growth.

The Chief Operating Officer works closely with the Minister for the Cabinet Office and the Chief Secretary to the Treasury to make sure there is a co-ordinated approach to tackling waste and improving accountability across all government departments.

Reporting to the Chief Executive of the Civil Service, the Government Chief Commercial Officer shapes and leads the programme of commercial reform across government.

The Government Chief Commercial Officer’s priorities include:

  • Leading the Government Commercial Function and Governments Grants Management Functions (GCF and GGMF)
  • To steer the cross-government commercial and grants communities in delivering the Plan for Change
  • Delivering efficiencies and savings through the use of digital, data and AI
  • Lead the Functional Strategy
  • Leads the relationship with HMG Strategic Suppliers
  • Improving contract delivery and contract management
  • Advancing the capability of the GCF and GGMF
  • Head of Profession, building a commercial and grants community across the UK

The Government Chief Commercial Officer works closely with departmental ministers, the Cabinet Secretary, departmental permanent secretaries and other senior officials, and senior leaders of government suppliers. The Government Chief Commercial Officer gives evidence to Parliamentary Select Committees, including the Public Accounts Committee, on commercial matters.

The Constitution Director supports the Deputy Prime Minister and the Minister for Political and Constitutional Reform on their responsibilities for law and policy on elections, referendums and political party funding.

The Director leads on related aspects of the political reform agenda set out in the Coalition Programme for Government. He or she works with the devolved administrations, territorial offices and Whitehall to maintain good relations. The Director is also responsible for:

  • overall devolution policy across government
  • electoral administration and registration
  • work to devolve power to cities
  • oversight of the honours system
  • the Privy Council Office

The Executive Director leads the Government Innovation Group, which works across Whitehall to develop and implement new ways of solving problems. It includes transparency and open data, analysis and insight, open policy making, behavioural insights and the Office for Civil Society.

His primary responsibility is leading the Civil Society agenda, in particular setting up the National Citizen Service for 16/17 year olds, encouraging social action and growing the social investment market by establishing Big Society Capital.

The National Security Adviser’s responsibilities include:

  • Providing advice to the Prime Minister and the Cabinet on national security matters. 
  • Attending the National Security Council.
  • Overseeing cross-government delivery of the National Security Strategy 2025.

The Joint Intelligence Committee (JIC) is a cross-government committee, based at the Cabinet Office, providing ministers and senior officials with co-ordinated inter-departmental intelligence assessments, primarily about security, defence and foreign affairs. It supports the National Security Council.

The Chair of the Joint Intelligence Committee is responsible to the Prime Minister for supervising the work of the JIC. The Chair is the head of the Joint Intelligence Organisation, which includes the Assessments Staff, a team of analysts responsible for drafting all-source intelligence assessments, and Professional Head of Intelligence Assessment.

ordered_roles.title: Chair of the Joint Intelligence Committee

details.body: The Prime Minister’s Adviser on Europe and Global Issues leads on European Union business. The adviser also leads on key global issues such as trade, energy, climate change, financial markets and international development.

ordered_roles.title: Prime Minister’s Adviser on Europe and Global Issues and Head of European and Global Issues Secretariat

details.body: The Director General in the Deputy Prime Minister’s Office supports the Deputy Prime Minister across the full range of his policy interests and is also responsible for the Constitution Group in the Cabinet Office.

ordered_roles.title: Director General, Deputy Prime Minister’s Office

details.body: Responsibilities of the Minister without Portfolio include contributing to the government’s policy and decision making processes.

The Minister for the Constitution supports the Minister for Cabinet Office in delivering Cabinet Office policy.

Responsibilities include:

  • elections and electoral registration
  • Parliamentary constituency boundaries
  • oversight of the devolution settlement
  • constitutional policy, including Crown affairs
  • political parties and referendums policy
  • democratic engagement
  • Public Appointments
  • FOI
  • Records and Archives
  • support to Minister for Cabinet Office on the functional agenda

The Minister for the Civil Service is responsible for regulating the Civil Service.

The Civil Service (Management Functions) Act of 1992, allows the Minister for the Civil Service to delegate power to other ministers and devolved administrations.

This role was created in 1968 and is always held by the Prime Minister.

The First Lord of the Treasury is one of the Lords Commissioners of the Treasury. This role is usually held by the Prime Minister.

Since the 17th century, the Lords Commissioners of the Treasury have collectively carried out duties that were previously held by the Lord High Treasurer (head of His Majesty’s Treasury).

The Lords Commissioners of the Treasury also include:

  • the Second Lord of the Treasury - the Chancellor of the Exchequer, who has most of the functional financial responsibilities
  • Junior Lords Commissioners of the Treasury - other members of the government, usually government whips in the House of Commons

10 Downing Street is the official residence of the First Lord of the Treasury, and not of the Prime Minister.

The minister is responsible for:

  • cities policy
  • local growth
  • political and constitutional reform

ordered_roles.title: Minister of State for Cabinet Office (Cities and Constitution)

details.body:
The Executive Director leads the Implementation Group and is responsible for:

  • helping the Prime Minister and Cabinet to track implementation of the government’s commitments
  • supporting the Implementation Task Forces to lead on implementing cross-cutting priorities
  • working to strengthen implementation capability across government
  • with the Better Regulation Executive, carrying out the Cutting Red Tape programme to reduce and improve the stock of regulation on the statute book and provide £10 billion of savings to UK businesses
Responsibilities for this role will be posted shortly.

ordered_roles.title: Minister of State for Cabinet Office

details.body:
The Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster administers the estates and rents of the Duchy of Lancaster, and is a member of the Cabinet.

He is responsible for:

  • Oversight of all Cabinet Office policy
  • Coordination and delivery of cross-government priorities
  • Oversight of national security policy coordination
  • Oversight of state reform and Civil Service reform
  • Communications policy for Government 
  • Propriety and ethics 
  • Public appointments

ordered_roles.title: Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster

details.body:
The minister is responsible for:

  • political and constitutional reform

The Chief Executive of the Civil Service leads the efficiency programme, working with permanent secretaries across government to put in place reforms including:

  • getting a better deal for taxpayers from commercial decisions and supplier management
  • the digital transformation of public services and the way government works
  • ensuring we have the best people with the right skills throughout the civil service
  • making better use of the government estate (property)
  • managing major projects better to deliver on time and on budget
  • greater use of shared services across departments

The Chief Executive sits on the Civil Service Board and attends Cabinet. The Chief Executive supports the Cabinet Secretary and Head of the Civil Service in performance-managing the other Whitehall permanent secretaries.

ordered_roles.title: Chief Executive of the Civil Service

details.body:
Non-Executive Board Members are appointed on merit, reflecting ability and experience, for a fixed term, usually three years. They will exercise their role through influence and advice, supporting as well as challenging the Executive. They will advise on the clarity of strategic direction, performance, operational issues and the effective management of the Department. These responsibilities should be conducted both in and outside of Board meetings - the Executive should invite and involve the Non-Executives in key areas to ensure critical challenge and support.

Non-Executives Board Members’ views on performance will be reported by the Lead Non-Executive Board Member in the Department’s Annual Report. Non-Executives’ views will also be fed back to the Prime Minister and the Government Lead Non-Executive through the Non-Executive Board Members’ Network.

Non-Executive Board Members may be involved in the process for recruiting and appraising senior executives, and succession planning.

The Parliamentary Secretary supports the Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster in ensuring that the government delivers its policy agenda.

The role also supports Cabinet Office business in the House of Lords.

The UK Governance Group was established in June 2015 to lead the UK government’s work on constitutional and devolution issues. It brings together under one command the Cabinet Office Constitution Group, the Scotland Office, the Office of the Advocate General for Scotland and the Wales Office.

The Group will ensure that the civil service has an improved capability to support ministers in the vital challenge of sustaining the United Kingdom and the constitutional settlement.

The Chief Operating Officer for the Civil Service is responsible for ensuring the high performance and efficiency of Government, and oversees the cross-cutting Government functions to build greater professionalism and expertise across the Civil Service. The Cabinet Office Permanent Secretary is the principal accounting officer and Civil Service head of the Cabinet Office, overseeing the department and ensuring it delivers for the Prime Minister, Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster, and wider Cabinet.

ordered_roles.title: Chief Operating Officer for the Civil Service and Permanent Secretary to the Cabinet Office

details.body: The First Parliamentary Counsel oversees the Government in Parliament Group, which includes Parliamentary Counsel and the Business Managers in each House of Parliament. The Group works with teams across government to prepare and deliver the government’s legislative programme.

Non-Executive Board Members are appointed on merit, reflecting ability and experience, for a fixed term, usually three years. They will exercise their role through influence and advice, supporting as well as challenging the Executive. They will advise on the clarity of strategic direction, performance, operational issues and the effective management of the Department. These responsibilities should be conducted both in and outside of Board meetings - the Executive should invite and involve the Non-Executives in key areas to ensure critical challenge and support.

Non-Executives Board Members’ views on performance will be reported by the Lead Non-Executive Board Member in the Department’s Annual Report. Non-Executives’ views will also be fed back to the Prime Minister and the Government Lead Non-Executive through the Non-Executive Board Members’ Network.

Non-Executive Board Members may be involved in the process for recruiting and appraising senior executives, and succession planning.

The Executive Director leads the Government Digital Service and is Head of Function for Digital, Data and Technology. The Executive Director is also a member of the Cabinet Office’s Executive Committee.

ordered_roles.title: Government's Special Envoy to the U.S. technology companies

details.body:
The Minister for the Constitution supports the Minister for Cabinet Office in delivering Cabinet Office policy.

Responsibilities include:

  • Constitution policy and democracy
  • FOI/transparency
  • Knowledge management
  • Public appointments
  • UK Statistics Authority
  • CO Parliamentary business
  • CO domestic
  • Fraud Error Debt and Grants, Government comms, Public Bodies Reform
The Director General leads the Government Digital Service and is Head of Function for Digital, Data and Technology. The Director General is also a member of the Cabinet Office’s Executive Committee.

Tim Hitchens is the Chief Executive of the Commonwealth Summit Unit. He is responsible for delivering the Commonwealth Heads of Government Meeting (CHOGM) 2018, working closely with the Commonwealth Secretariat and member states. His dedicated unit, based in the Cabinet Office, will report directly to the Prime Minister.

The UK Governance Group was established in June 2015 to lead the UK government’s work on constitution and devolution. It brings together under one command the Cabinet Office’s Constitution Group, the Scotland Office, the Office of the Advocate General for Scotland and the Wales Office. The group ensures that the civil service has an improved capability to support ministers in the vital challenge of sustaining the United Kingdom and the constitutional settlement.

The UK Governance Group’s objectives are:

  • Achieve a stable, fair and enduring constitutional framework for the United Kingdom;
  • Sustain a flourishing democracy; and
  • Maintain the integrity of the United Kingdom.

• Advising the Prime Minister on developing and implementing Government policy
  • Driving forward government business and implementation including through chairing and deputy chairing cabinet committees and taskforces
  • Overseeing devolution consequences of EU exit
  • Overseeing constitutional affairs and maintaining the integrity of the Union
  • Oversight of all Cabinet Office policies

• Supporting MCO on cross-government delivery and implementation
  • Controls (commercial, digital, property)
  • Commercial models
  • Cyber and resilience
  • Civil Service HR and Shared Services
  • Fraud, Error, Debt and Grants
  • Geospatial Commission
  • Government Digital Service
  • Government Security Group
  • Infrastructure and Projects Authority
  • Government Property
  • Government Commercial Function
  • Public bodies and appointments policy

The Minster for Government Resilience and Efficiency supports the Minister for the Cabinet Office in delivering Cabinet Office policy. Responsibilities include:

  • efficiency and controls
  • functions - Infrastructure and Projects Authority, Civil Service HR, the Government Digital Service, commercial and Crown Commercial Service, Government Property and commercial models
  • shared services
  • Civil Service workforce issues
  • Single Departmental Plans
  • cyber
  • resilience

ordered_roles.title: Minister for Government Resilience and Efficiency

details.body:
The Constitution Group sits within UK Governance Group supporting the First Secretary of State and other ministers in delivering the Government’s constitutional and devolution agenda.

Constitution Group’s functions include:

  • Electoral law
  • Policy and democratic machinery of the UK
  • The devolution settlements in Scotland and Wales
  • Constitutional issues such as House of Lords reform and English Votes for English Laws
  • Ensuring effective intergovernmental relations
  • Modern Electoral Registration Programme & ownership of the register to vote digital service

• give the Prime Minister the best possible advice on how to lead the Government’s approach

  • support coherent and timely cross-Government decision-making

  • work with other departments to advocate and deliver the Government’s position with European partners

The Special Representative will support the delivery of the government’s objective to make the UK the safest place to use the internet without fear of abuse, criminality or exposure to harmful content. The Special Representative will support the Prime Minister, Home Secretary and Culture Secretary at home and abroad.

The Special Representative will:

  • support the government’s work on internet safety representing the UK in the US, EU and international forums, conferences and events. This includes on-going programmes of work and commitments with internet companies internationally (for example, through the EU, ​US, Canada, Australia, New Zealand and other​ international partner​s).
  • encourage the development of new company-led interventions to improve internet safety and to address abuse, criminality and harmful content online.
  • engage with established internet platform providers and facilitate their support of smaller companies with less established practices to combat internet abuse and harms.
  • continue serving on the WePROTECT Board and supporting the WePROTECT international agenda and expansion.

The Special Representative will be based in the Cabinet Office and be unpaid.

ordered_roles.title: Prime Minister's Special Representative on Internet Safety

details.body:
Responsibilities include:

  • constitutional policy and democracy
  • propriety, ethics and transparency
  • knowledge management
  • UK Statistics Authority
  • Cabinet Office Parliamentary business
  • Cabinet Office domestic (including Cabinet Office HR, finance and security)
  • government communications
The Minister without Portfolio is a member of Cabinet.

The Deputy Director of Business Partnerships is responsible for:

  • delivering business expertise into government

  • creating partnerships between businesses and the government to help deliver policy innovation

  • helping businesses navigate government and connect to the relevant Whitehall teams

Non-Executive Board Members are appointed on merit, reflecting ability and experience, for a fixed term, usually three years. They will exercise their role through influence and advice, supporting as well as challenging the Executive. They will advise on the clarity of strategic direction, performance, operational issues and the effective management of the Department. These responsibilities should be conducted both in and outside of Board meetings - the Executive should invite and involve the Non-Executives in key areas to ensure critical challenge and support.

Non-Executives Board Members’ views on performance will be reported by the Lead Non-Executive Board Member in the Department’s Annual Report. Non-Executives’ views will also be fed back to the Prime Minister and the Government Lead Non-Executive through the Non-Executive Board Members’ Network.

Non-Executive Board Members may be involved in the process for recruiting and appraising senior executives, and succession planning.

Cabinet Office and Cabinet Office Board

The Government Chief Security Officer (GCSO) leads the Government Security Function, which seeks to build the capacity and capabilities of security professionals across UK government departments, covering physical, human and information security.

The GCSO is also Director General of the Government Security Group within the Cabinet Office, which is responsible for the oversight, coordination and delivery of protective security within all central government departments, their agencies and arms-length bodies.

The GCSO is also responsible for, and heads, the Government Security Profession, bringing together security professionals from across government in supporting them gain skills and knowledge to fulfil their roles.

• Constitutional policy and democracy
  • Integrity of the Union
  • Devolution consequences of Brexit
  • FOI and transparency

  • Cabinet Office parliamentary business, including Brexit preparations

details.body:

  • civil, family and criminal justice systems
  • prisons, probation and youth justice

  • supporting the Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster to strengthen the Union

ordered_roles.title: Minister of State (Minister for the Constitution and Devolution)

details.body:
Responsibilities include:

  • leads on all Cabinet Office business in the Lords, including the Cabinet Office legislation programme in the Lords
  • supporting the Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster on constitutional issues
  • supporting Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster on cross-cutting domestic and economic priorities

Responsibilities include supporting the Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster and the Chief Secretary to the Treasury to deliver cross-government efficiency and public sector transformation improvements, including:

  • supporting the Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster and the Chief Secretary to the Treasury to deliver cross-government and public sector reform and efficiency, including public value and planning and performance
  • supporting the Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster with oversight of the corporate functions and operation of the spending controls, including in relation to COVID-19
  • HMT Lords Spokesperson

ordered_roles.title: Minister of State (Minister for Efficiency and Transformation)

details.body:
Responsibilities include:

  • supporting the Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster and Lord Agnew to deliver cross-government efficiency and public sector transformation
  • supporting Lord Frost on domestic transition readiness and opportunities flowing from EU exit
  • supporting Cabinet Office primary legislation in the Commons and the Cabinet Office secondary legislation programme
  • oversight of Cabinet Office corporate activity, including staff within the Equalities Hub (responsibility for equalities policy sits with Minister for Women & Equalities, Minister for Disabled People and Exchequer Secretary to the Treasury)
  • the GREAT campaign
As Minister for the Union, the Prime Minister works to ensure that all of government is acting on behalf of the entire United Kingdom: England, Northern Ireland, Scotland, and Wales.
The Chief Operating Officer for the Civil Service leads the efficiency programme, working with permanent secretaries across government to put in place reforms.

ordered_roles.title: Chief Operating Officer for the Civil Service

details.body:
Non-Executive Board Members are appointed on merit, reflecting ability and experience, for a fixed term, usually three years. They will exercise their role through influence and advice, supporting as well as challenging the Executive. They will advise on the clarity of strategic direction, performance, operational issues and the effective management of the Department. These responsibilities should be conducted both in and outside of Board meetings - the Executive should invite and involve the Non-Executives in key areas to ensure critical challenge and support.

Non-Executives Board Members’ views on performance will be reported by the Lead Non-Executive Board Member in the Department’s Annual Report. Non-Executives’ views will also be fed back to the Prime Minister and the Government Lead Non-Executive through the Non-Executive Board Members’ Network.

Non-Executive Board Members may be involved in the process for recruiting and appraising senior executives, and succession planning.

The Deputy Cabinet Secretary is responsible for the management of the Cabinet Secretariat including advising on the operation of the Cabinet and collective government, machinery of government, the Codes and Cabinet Manual; propriety & ethics, the Independent Offices and public appointments & public bodies.

Responsibilities include:

  • leading preparations for the 26th United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change Conference of the Parties (COP26), to be held in Glasgow in November 2021.
  • chairing the Climate Action Implementation Cabinet Committee to coordinate government action towards net zero by 2050

The CEO is responsible for the strategic direction, and operational excellence of GDS. They ensure the delivery of world-class digital services that transform the way citizens interact with the government.

ordered_roles.title: Chief Executive Officer, Government Digital Service

• oversight of the implementation of the Trade and Cooperation Agreement and Withdrawal Agreement, including the Northern Ireland protocol
  • UK co-chair of the Joint Committee and Partnership Council

  • oversight of the development of cross-Government work on maximising the economic and political opportunities flowing from EU exit

ordered_roles.title: Parliamentary Secretary (Minister for Defence People and Veterans)

The Chief Operating Officer is responsible for the following areas in the Cabinet Office:

  • Analysis and Insight
  • Assurance, Finance and Control (including Security and Commercial)

The Second Permanent Secretary leads on the Union and the Constitution, responsible for both the Union Directorate and the UK Governance Group.

The Second Permanent Secretary is also the sponsor for the Grenfell Tower and Infected Blood Independent Public Inquiries.

ordered_roles.title: Second Permanent Secretary to Cabinet Office

details.body:
The Government Lead Non-Executive works with Secretaries of State to:

  • appoint non-executives to the board of each government department
  • improve governance across Whitehall
  • build leadership and management through the non-executives and the boards

The Government Lead Non-Executive also convenes network meetings of non-executives across Whitehall to consider the big issues that challenge all government departments and share best practice.

• Deputy National Security Adviser and Prime Minister’s Adviser on National Resilience and Security in the Cabinet Office
  • Wellbeing champion for the Cabinet Office

ordered_roles.title: Deputy National Security Adviser and Prime Minister's Adviser on National Resilience and Security in the Cabinet Office

details.body:
The Director of People and Places is responsible for Cabinet Office Human Resources and Estates. Responsibilities include:

  • managing the strategic estate requirements of the Cabinet Office, overseeing delivery partners in the Government Property Agency (GPA)
  • leading the design and implementation of the department’s locations strategy - a transformational people change programme to increase the presence of the department across regional and devolved locations
  • leading transformational reforms in the Department, such as the transition to smarter, hybrid ways of working

The Director of Communications has accountability for all internal and external communications within the Cabinet Office. The role’s responsibilities include:

  • overseeing internal communications within the department and across the Civil Service, including flagship events such as Civil Service Live and other departmental teams such as the press office, media monitoring and rebuttal, and external affairs
  • providing senior counsel to the Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster, Cabinet Office ministers and other senior officials
  • working across government to deliver the department’s priorities, including priority campaigns such as Civil Service reform, EU Exit, and protecting the Union
  • contributing to the leadership of the department, including reporting to and deputising for the Executive Director of Government Communications

The CFO is responsible for financial management assurance across the department, striving to ensure that the Cabinet Office gets the best return on its resources.

The CFO leads the Commercial and Portfolio function in the Cabinet Office. The CFO also manages the Security teams within the Cabinet Office, the Cabinet Office controls team as well the departmental enterprise platform.

The CFO’s policy remit is to convene the work on functional reform and impact and professional standards.

The Director General is responsible for the Cabinet Office teams covering the following areas:

  • support for Lord Frost and the Prime Minister on the effective conduct and coordination of EU related business with the EU and its Member States
  • the work across HM Government to ensure the effective implementation of the Trade and Cooperation Agreement and Withdrawal Agreement, including the Northern Ireland protocol
  • the major UK-EU committees established to manage the Trade and Cooperation Agreement and Withdrawal Agreement including the Joint Committee and Partnership Council

ordered_roles.title: Member of the Committee on Standards in Public Life
Member of the Committee on Standards in Public Life

Responsibilities include:

  • supporting and advising Ministers and the Permanent Secretary
  • helping to make sure the Cabinet Office runs effectively and delivers its objectives
  • serving on the Cabinet Office Executive Committee.

• supporting the Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster on oversight of Public Appointments

• deliver the UK’s commitment as part of the G7 pledge made by Leaders at the Summit in June to aim for a step change in approaches to infrastructure financing

  • writerounds

Responsibilities:

  • Supporting CDL on driving the delivery of Government’s priorities
  • GREAT campaign
  • Geospatial Commission
  • Office of Government Property
  • Government Property Agency
  • Places for Growth
  • Conflict Stability and Security Fund (CSSF)
  • Government Communications Service

ordered_roles.title: Minister of State for the Investment Security Unit

details.body: The Chief Product and Technology Officer for the Government Digital Service (GDS) is responsible for the digital professions at GDS as well as our Digital Service Platforms and Software Engineering.

ordered_roles.title: Adviser to the Prime Minister and Deputy Prime Minister on Artificial Intelligence

details.body:
Non-Executive Board Members are appointed on merit, reflecting ability and experience, for a fixed term, usually three years. They will exercise their role through influence and advice, supporting as well as challenging the Executive. They will advise on the clarity of strategic direction, performance, operational issues and the effective management of the Department. These responsibilities should be conducted both in and outside of Board meetings - the Executive should invite and involve the Non-Executives in key areas to ensure critical challenge and support.

Non-Executives Board Members’ views on performance will be reported by the Lead Non-Executive Board Member in the Department’s Annual Report. Non-Executives’ views will also be fed back to the Prime Minister and the Government Lead Non-Executive through the Non-Executive Board Members’ Network.

Non-Executive Board Members may be involved in the process for recruiting and appraising senior executives, and succession planning.

Supporting the Deputy Prime Minister on:

  • Driving delivery of government’s priorities
  • Ensuring effective communication of government’s priorities

Supporting the Deputy Prime Minister and the Minister for the Cabinet Office on:

  • Ensuring efficiency and value for money in government policy
  • Ensuring efficiency and value for money in government delivery 

Supporting the Deputy Prime Minister and Baroness Neville-Rolfe on:

  • Public appointments outreach

Supporting the Minister for the Cabinet Office on:

  • Public Bodies reform programme

• Driving delivery of government’s priorities
  • Oversight of all Cabinet Office policy 
  • Oversight of civil contingencies and resilience
  • National security and cyber security
  • Economic security, including National Security and Investment Act
  • Oversight of Cabinet Office business planning 
  • Oversight of major events
  • Propriety and ethics 
  • Public appointments
  • Honours
  • GREAT campaign

Responsibilities include:

  • Investment Security Unit

She is responsible for:

  • Missions - supporting delivery of Government’s priorities
  • GREAT campaign

The Minister for the Cabinet Office is the deputy to the Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster in all departmental business, including on inter-governmental relations and public appointments.

He is responsible for:

  • EU Policy and relations including the Windsor Framework
  • Oversight of functions and services
  • Civil Service reform
  • Inquiries policy 
  • Infected blood inquiry 
  • Duty of candour
  • Constitution and House of Lords reform 
  • Cabinet Office business planning and performance 
  • Communications function & reform
  • Public sector workforce

ordered_roles.title: Minister for the Cabinet Office (Minister for the Constitution and European Union Relations)

details.body:
She is responsible for:

  • Public sector reform
  • Oversight of government functions
  • Cabinet Office business planning and performance
  • Public Bodies policy
  • Cabinet Office arm’s length body sponsorship

She is responsible for:

  • National security, resilience, and civil contingencies, including:
    • Integrated Security Fund
    • Government Security Group, including UK Security Vetting
  • Transparency Data policy, correspondence policy, and Freedom of Information
  • Supporting the Minister for the Cabinet Office on Inquiries policy and constitution

As Minister for Intergovernmental Relations, the role holder leads coordination with the devolved administrations on the Prime Minister’s behalf, working closely with the Territorial Offices, to make sure that across government work is being done on behalf of the entire United Kingdom: England, Northern Ireland, Scotland, and Wales.

This role oversees the Domestic and Economic Pillar, including the Economic and Domestic Affairs Secretariat and 10 Data Science - advising the Prime Minister and Chief Secretary to the Prime Minister on economic and domestic affairs.

Responsibilities include:

  • Supporting the Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster on Intergovernmental Relations

ordered_roles.title: Minister of State at the Cabinet Office

details.body:
Responsibilities include:

  • trade strategy 
  • free trade agreement (FTA) negotiations
  • UK-EU Trade and Cooperation Agreement (TCA) Implementation
  • Digital Trade Agreements
  • UK Conformity Assessment (UKCA)
  • Mutual Recognition Agreements
  • Windsor Framework
  • investor treaties and disputes
  • investor screening
  • UK Internal Market 
  • trade defence
  • bilateral trade relations
  • World Trade Organization (WTO), G7, G20, Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) and Commonwealth
  • trade remedies
  • tariffs and disputes 
  • global supply chains
  • economic security
  • Export Control Joint Unit
  • sanctions

The Minister of State is responsible for the following public bodies:

  • Trade Remedies Authority

ordered_roles.title: Minister of State (Minister for Trade Policy and Economic Security) at the Department for Business and Trade

details.body: The Chief Secretary to the Prime Minister oversees work across Government to support the delivery of the Prime Minister’s priorities and the Government’s Plan for Change.

ordered_roles.title: Chief Secretary to the Prime Minister

details.body:
The Minister’s responsibilities are:

  • Supporting CDL with coordination of national security policy
  • Resilience 
  • Civil contingencies
  • Integrated security fund
  • Investment security
  • Cyber security
  • Government security group, including UK security vetting

She is responsible for supporting the Chief Secretary to the Prime Minister and the Minister for the Cabinet Office on

  • Public bodies
  • Government property function and the Places for Growth programme
  • Government people group

The Parliamentary Secretary is responsible for supporting the Chief Secretary to the Prime Minister and the Minister for the Cabinet Office on coordination and delivery of cross-government priorities.

In particular, the Minister’s responsibilities are to support the Chief Secretary to the Prime Minister and the Minister for the Cabinet Office on:

  • EU Reset and the Windsor Framework
  • Government commercial function
  • Infrastructure delivery
  • Union and constitutional matters
  • Fiscal events and departmental business planning
  • Transparency and freedom of information policy

The role of the Cabinet Office Lead NEBM is to provide strategic oversight and leadership for the department’s team of Non-Executives. The Lead NEBM supports ministers and officials by providing expert advice and challenge on delivery and performance. As well as their formal role on the Board and its sub-committees, the Lead NEBM also maintains close working relationships with the Permanent Secretary and the Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster to support the delivery of their priorities. 

The Lead NEBM works with the department to ensure the NEBMs are assigned to work on issues where they will have the most impact and can best support the delivery of the department’s strategic priorities.

The Cabinet Office Audit and Risk Committee offers an independent perspective on the department’s financial, risk, and control arrangements. It also reviews and recommends the approval of accounts for the Cabinet Office, Office of the Registrar of Consultant Lobbyists, Civil Superannuation, and the Royal Mail Statutory Pension Scheme.

ordered_roles.title: Audit and Risk Committee Chair and Non-Executive Board Member

details.body:
The Cabinet Office Board provides strategic leadership for the department, comprising Cabinet Office ministers, senior executives, and non-executives from outside government. Its purpose is to advise on strategy, monitor performance, and assess significant risks.

The Non-Executive Board Members are responsible for providing support and challenge to the department’s ministers and senior officials on the delivery of key policies and programmes.

The Cabinet Office Board provides strategic leadership for the department, comprising Cabinet Office ministers, senior executives, and non-executives from outside government. Its purpose is to advise on strategy, monitor performance, and assess significant risks.

The Non-Executive Board Members are responsible for providing support and challenge to the department’s ministers and senior officials on the delivery of key policies and programmes.

The Permanent Secretary for Communications is responsible for transforming how the UK Government communicates and builds trust with the public. It is also the head of the Government Communications profession.

Responsibilities include:

  1. Delivering a government communication strategy that reflects the evolving media landscape and technological changes.
  2. Leading major cross-government communications to support the Prime Minister’s Plan for Change, ensuring the public gets clear, timely and engaging information.
  3. Modernising how the government communicates by building capability, driving innovation and ensuring that Government Communications operates effectively and efficiently.
Director General for the Economic and Domestic Secretariat (EDS) at Cabinet Office

title: Cabinet Office
